checks.a_analysis: The output content was meticulously compared with the source PDF. All headers, list items, and descriptions in the output correspond to the content present in the source document. No fabricated sentences or paragraphs were found.

The conference bridge phone number '888-808-6929' and ID '8153047#' were verified against the source and are correct. All physical addresses (109 East North Street, 3514 Bush Street) are accurately transcribed. No fabricated contact information exists.

The document contains two pages in the source and the output. All items from the agenda (1 through 13), the committee meetings schedule, and the board meeting details from page 2 are present in the output.

The source document uses a layout with headers 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'. The output captures the first two entries (Call to Order, Roll Call) in a table and the remaining entries (1-13) in a structured list. While the 'table' element in the output has fewer rows than the visual columns in the source, all information from those rows (Topic and Presenter names) is fully preserved in the subsequent list structure, which is an acceptable accessibility reformatting.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All fund balances and disbursement figures for the NG 911, CMRS, PSAP, and Grant funds were checked for accuracy and completeness. All Peer Reviewer names (Vicki Callicutt, Megan Daub, Greg Dotson, Mark Reavis, Tracy Trogdon) are present. No significant text omissions were identified.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording across the document is accurate. Names, dates (January 18, 2019; February 22, 2019), and financial values match the source exactly. Minor differences such as joining columns with a hyphen (e.g., 'Chairman’s Opening Remarks - Eric Boyette') are acceptable reformatting to maintain semantic meaning in a list.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents the content of the source PDF once. No repeated sections, paragraphs, or list items were found that were not present in the original document.

The document heading hierarchy is broken. The output uses Heading Level 3 (H3) for the primary meeting title ('NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING') and for major section titles ('FEBRUARY 2019 COMMITTEE MEETINGS'), skipping levels H1 and H2 entirely. This results in a non-standard hierarchy that hampers navigation for screen reader users.
checks.b_evidence_source: NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING (Page 1), FEBRUARY 2019 COMMITTEE MEETINGS (Page 2)
checks.b_evidence_output: "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING" (element index 1); "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "FEBRUARY 2019 COMMITTEE MEETINGS" (element index 7)

checks.a_analysis: The source document presents the entire agenda as a three-column table with headers 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'. This structure persists from 'Call to Order' through item '13. Other'. The output JSON incorrectly identifies only the first two rows as a table and then transitions into a list for items 1-13. Crucially, it merges data from the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' columns into single text blocks (e.g., 'Chairman’s Opening Remarks - Eric Boyette'), losing the column-based structural organization of the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: The three-column table starting with 'Tab Topic Presenter' headers on page 1 and continuing through page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table element (element index 3) only contains 'Call to Order' and 'Roll Call'. Subsequent rows (1-13) are converted to a 'list' element (element index 4) with merged column content.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom, page-by-page flow of the source document.

The nested list structures (e.g., item 4 with sub-items a-h, and 4.g with sub-items i-v) are correctly preserved in terms of hierarchy and nesting, despite the outer structure being incorrectly changed from a table to a list (noted in S3).

The source document contains a clear hyperlink in red text: '20190111 NC 911 Board WebEx Link'. This link is represented as plain text in the output JSON paragraph and is not identified as a functional link object.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Friday, January 18, 2019\nWebEx and Conference Bridge\n20190111 NC 911 Board WebEx Link..."

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-column layout of the agenda is successfully translated into a combination of a table for the initial items and a structured list for the numbered items. Presenter names, which were in a separate column in the PDF, are clearly associated with their respective topics in the HTML (e.g., using a dash or within the table). The logical flow of information remains consistent.

The table under 'Agenda Items and Presenters' in the HTML is correctly rendered with visible borders and aligned columns, reflecting the 'Call to Order' and 'Roll Call' rows from the PDF. The financial data sections (4b-4e), while visually aligned in the PDF using whitespace, are rendered as list items in the HTML. Although the multi-line alignment is lost as they are condensed into paragraphs within list items, the data remains readable and the structure is coherent.

The primary meaningful image from the source, the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo, is present and correctly rendered in the HTML output. Decorative or bullet-point glyphs used in the PDF are replaced with standard HTML list markers, which is an appropriate conversion.

No body content (paragraphs, list items, or tables) is duplicated within the HTML page. Section headings appearing in both the Table of Contents and the document body is an intentional accessibility feature and not a defect.
